Notice Of Motion And Motion For Award Of Monetary Sanctions And Issue Sanctions For Failure To Respond To Interrogatories And Request For Production Of Documents
Off Calendar Aug-15-2005 Off Calendar

Ntc Of Mtn & Mtn To Compel Pltf To Submit To An Independent Mental Examination & Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Aug-01-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCE. Plaintiff's motion to compel an independent mental examination: The motion is ordered off calendar at the request of the moving party. (610/BCHAN)

Ntc Of Mtn & Mtn To Compel Pltf To Submit To An Independent Mental Examination & Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jun-29-2005 Continued To Aug-01-05 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 610
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Plaintiff's motion to compel an independent mental examination is continued on the Court's own motion to August 1, 2005 at 9:00 a.m. The parties shall advise the Court if the plaintiff appeared for the examination before the next scheduled appearance.

Pursuant to the prior order of the Court, the Defendants submitted the medical records for in camera review on June 17, 2005. The Court has finished its review and declines to order production of pages 115 - 125. Counsel for plaintiff shall contact the clerk to obtain copies of the records. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

Plaintiff'S Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatoires, Request For Production Of Documents And Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar May-11-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE ADOPTED: Plaintiff's motion to compel further Responses -
Form interrogatories
12.1: Granted. The objections are overruled and stricken. Defendant is ordered to respond further. It is insufficient to refer plaintiff to the medical records. For any persons who are currently employed by defendant, they may be identified by the individual's name and work address.

Special interrogatories
1: Granted. The objections are overruled and stricken. The interrogatory is rewritten to ask for the name, address, job description and job title of the person or persons most knowledgeable of the policies and procedures which were in effect as of January 28, 2003, on the ward in which plaintiff was housed, regarding staff responsibilities to monitor the safety of patients and to prevent misconduct by patients.
2: Granted. The objections are overruled and stricken. Defendant is ordered to respond further. It is insufficient to refer plaintiff to the medical records. For any persons who are currently employed by defendant, they may be identified by the individual's name and work address.
3, 5, 6 : Granted. The objections are overruled and stricken.
4: Granted. The objections are overruled and stricken. The question does not implicate either the attorney - client or the attorney work product privilege.

Request for production
1: The parties are ordered to meet and confer to narrow the request by further defining the term "monitoring"
2: Denied as moot
4: Granted. The objections, other than the right to privacy, are overruled and stricken. Any identifying information as to alleged victims is ordered redacted.
7: Granted in part. Defendant is ordered to produce the records for in camera review. The records are to be bates-stamped to enable the Court to make a record of any documents that are ordered withheld. Counsel for plaintiff shall also provide the Court with a copy of the relevant police incident report. (610/BCHAN)

MOTION Compeling Responses To Interrogatories & Request For Production Of Documents For Award Of Monetary Sanctions
